name: requirement_list_state_guide
description: Walk a client-supplied requirement list in array order. Unlike agent_req_state_guide (no args first; state = checklist id), HERE the user MUST supply requirements on the first call. First call: pass non-empty requirements array (objects with id, name, depends_on, summary, rationale, inputs, outputs, functional_requirements, acceptance_criteria, test_scope); omit current_state. Later calls: pass current_state ONLY, set to continuation_state from the prior response (requirements ignored). Agent procedure: start the sequence and keep calling back-to-back until is_end with id end_requirement_list, or state error—do not abandon the walk mid-sequence. For EACH requirement before terminal: run full agent REQ checklist once (agent_req_state_guide through end_agent_req), strict TDD. Terminal: synthetic id end_requirement_list, is_end true; bad token or empty list returns state error.

requirements.description: Requirement objects in walk order. REQUIRED on first call when current_state is omitted or empty.

current_state.description: Opaque continuation_state from the previous response. When set, requirements is ignored; use to get the next requirement.
